title: The AI Boom: Economic Growth and Rising Social Tensions
subtitle: As investment in artificial intelligence reaches record levels, the technology is sparking both financial excitement and dangerous new forms of public anger.

summary.en: The artificial intelligence industry is experiencing massive financial growth, with spending expected to reach 1.6 trillion dollars by 2031. While companies and the public are adopting AI tools at a record pace, the rapid expansion is causing significant social friction. Experts warn that the industry's influence is now fueling new forms of political violence and extremist activity. Local communities are also pushing back against the environmental and infrastructure costs of new data centers. The future of this technology depends on whether companies can prove its real-world value while managing these growing public concerns.
summary.zh: 人工智慧產業正經歷巨大的財務成長，預計到二零三一年支出將達到一點六兆美元。儘管企業與大眾正以破紀錄的速度採用人工智慧工具，但這種快速擴張也引發了顯著的社會摩擦。專家警告，該產業的影響力正助長新型態的政治暴力與極端活動。地方社區也開始反對新資料中心帶來的環境與基礎設施成本。這項技術的未來，取決於企業能否在管理這些日益增長的公眾疑慮的同時，證明其現實世界的價值。

paragraphs.en: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence continues to reshape the global economy, but this progress comes with increasing social and financial risks. Recent data shows that investment in AI is moving at a staggering rate. According to Goldman Sachs, spending on AI infrastructure—such as the massive data centers required to power these systems—is expected to grow from 765 billion dollars this year to 1.6 trillion dollars by 2031. This surge has turned the stock market into what some experts call a giant AI-focused structure, where a small group of technology companies now accounts for nearly half of the total market value of the S&P 500.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧的快速成長持續重塑全球經濟，但這種進步也伴隨著日益增加的社會與財務風險。近期數據顯示，人工智慧領域的投資正以驚人的速度成長。根據高盛集團（Goldman Sachs）的報告，人工智慧基礎設施的支出，例如維持這些系統運作所需的大型資料中心，預計將從今年的七千六百五十億美元，成長至二零三一年的一點六兆美元。這股熱潮已使股市演變成某些專家口中所謂的「人工智慧導向結構」，其中少數幾家科技公司目前佔據了標準普爾五百指數（S&P 500）總市值的近半數。

paragraphs.en: Despite this massive financial commitment, analysts remain cautious. Neil Wilson, an analyst at the investment platform Saxo UK, warns that the current market looks like a dangerous bubble. He suggests that if companies cannot prove that AI reduces costs and improves work results, the industry could face a crash similar to the dotcom bubble of the past. Currently, while companies are adopting AI at a high rate—rising from 33 percent in 2023 to nearly 80 percent today—there is still a long way to go before these tools become truly profitable and efficient for most businesses.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管投入了龐大的資金，分析師仍持審慎態度。投資平台盛寶銀行英國分部（Saxo UK）的分析師尼爾·威爾森（Neil Wilson）警告，目前的市場看起來像是一個危險的泡沫。他認為，如果企業無法證明人工智慧能有效降低成本並提升工作績效，該產業可能會面臨類似過去網路泡沫化的崩盤。目前，雖然企業採用人工智慧的比率極高，從二零二三年的百分之三十三上升至今日的近百分之八十，但這些工具要對多數企業真正產生獲利與效率，仍有一段漫長的路要走。

paragraphs.en: As the technology spreads, the competition between AI developers is also intensifying. While OpenAI’s ChatGPT remains the most popular tool with one billion monthly users, other companies are gaining ground. Anthropic’s Claude has seen its usage grow significantly, especially after it introduced tools that allow AI to perform tasks without human help. According to data from the internet analysis company Kentik, Claude’s user traffic grew faster than its competitors between January and April of this year. This shift shows how quickly the industry is moving toward autonomous systems that can work independently.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著技術普及，人工智慧開發商之間的競爭也日益激烈。雖然 OpenAI 的 ChatGPT 仍是以每月十億用戶數穩居最受歡迎的工具，但其他公司也正急起直追。Anthropic 公司的 Claude 使用量顯著成長，特別是在該公司推出能讓人工智慧在無人類協助下執行任務的工具之後。根據網路分析公司 Kentik 的數據，今年一月至四月間，Claude 的用戶流量成長速度超越了競爭對手。這種轉變顯示出該產業正迅速朝向能獨立運作的自主系統發展。

paragraphs.en: However, this rapid rollout is causing more than just economic debate; it is fueling a new and dangerous wave of anti-tech extremism. Researchers and law enforcement officials have expressed alarm over a series of attacks directed at tech companies and their leaders. In one recent case, a man was arrested for allegedly trying to burn down the headquarters of OpenAI. In another instance, an Indianapolis city councilor found his home targeted by gunfire, with a note left behind that read, "NO DATA CENTERS."
paragraphs.zh: 然而，這種快速推廣所引發的不僅是經濟層面的辯論，更助長了一股危險的新型反科技極端主義浪潮。研究人員與執法官員對一系列針對科技公司及其領導者的攻擊事件表示擔憂。在近期的一起案例中，一名男子因涉嫌企圖縱火燒毀 OpenAI 總部而被捕。另一起案例中，印第安納波利斯（Indianapolis）的一位市議員住家遭到槍擊，現場留下一張寫著「拒絕資料中心」的字條。

paragraphs.en: Jordyn Abrams, a researcher at the Program on Extremism at George Washington University, notes that AI is becoming a driver of political violence. This is a new phenomenon, where different extremist groups—from those worried about mass government surveillance to those with environmental concerns—are using anti-AI language to justify their actions. Yannick Veilleux-Lepage, an associate professor at the Royal Military College of Canada, explains that this movement crosses traditional political lines. Many people feel that the technology is changing society too quickly, leaving no time for communities to adapt or build resilience against the disruption.
paragraphs.zh: 喬治華盛頓大學（George Washington University）極端主義計畫的研究員喬丹·艾布拉姆斯（Jordyn Abrams）指出，人工智慧正成為政治暴力的驅動因素。這是一種新現象，不同的極端團體，從擔憂政府大規模監控者到關注環境議題者，都利用反人工智慧的言論來合理化其行為。加拿大皇家軍事學院（Royal Military College of Canada）副教授雅尼克·維約-勒帕熱（Yannick Veilleux-Lepage）解釋，這場運動跨越了傳統的政治界線。許多人認為這項技術改變社會的速度太快，導致社區沒有時間適應或建立對抗這種破壞的韌性。

paragraphs.en: This backlash is not entirely new. Historically, major technological changes have often been met with resistance from those who feel left behind or threatened. Just as 19th-century workers once destroyed machines that they believed were taking their jobs, today’s critics are targeting the physical infrastructure of the AI age. Beyond the threat of violence, many local communities are organizing to stop the construction of new data centers, which require vast amounts of electricity and water. These local efforts often conflict with the national goals of tech companies and government planners who want to lead the world in AI development.
paragraphs.zh: 這種反彈並非全然新鮮事。從歷史來看，重大的技術變革往往會遭到那些感到被遺落或受到威脅的人們抵制。正如十九世紀的勞工曾摧毀他們認為會搶走工作的機器一樣，今日的批評者正將目標對準人工智慧時代的實體基礎設施。除了暴力威脅外，許多地方社區也正組織起來，試圖阻止新資料中心的建設，因為這些設施需要消耗大量的電力與水資源。這些地方性的努力，往往與那些希望在人工智慧發展上領先全球的科技公司及政府規劃者的國家目標產生衝突。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the AI industry faces a critical test. Companies must demonstrate that their technology provides real benefits to society, rather than just causing disruption and environmental strain. As Goldman Sachs analysts noted, even small delays in building data centers could lead to serious questions about the demand for these expensive projects. If the industry cannot find a way to balance its massive growth with the needs and safety of the public, the current boom may face a difficult and uncertain future.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，人工智慧產業正面臨一項關鍵考驗。企業必須證明其技術能為社會帶來實質利益，而非僅是造成破壞與環境負擔。正如高盛分析師所指出的，即便在建設資料中心時出現小幅延宕，也可能引發外界對這些昂貴計畫需求量的嚴重質疑。如果該產業無法找到方法，在龐大成長與公眾需求及安全之間取得平衡，當前的榮景可能會面臨艱難且不確定的未來。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make up a particular amount or part of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於解釋原因，這裡指佔據市場份額。文中：a small group of technology companies now accounts for nearly half of the total market value of the S&P 500.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become more popular, successful, or advanced in a competition.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 取得進展；越來越受歡迎；佔上風。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思容易誤解為「獲得土地」，實際指競爭中取得優勢。文中：other companies are gaining ground.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase or strengthen something, especially something negative like anger or conflict.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 加劇；助長（負面情緒或衝突）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（燃料），這裡作動詞，形容加劇了某種趨勢。文中：it is fueling a new and dangerous wave of anti-tech extremism.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A person or thing that causes something to happen or develop.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指「司機」，這裡指導致某事發生的因素。文中：Jordyn Abrams, a researcher at the Program on Extremism at George Washington University, notes that AI is becoming a driver of political violence.

previousArticles.title: The AI Boom: Progress, Profits, and Growing Public Concern
previousArticles.summaryEn: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence is changing the global economy and how companies operate. While investors are pouring billions into the technology, the industry faces serious questions about its real-world effectiveness. Recent legal challenges highlight failures in AI safety systems meant to protect public spaces. Meanwhile, the fast pace of development is causing social tension and even political violence. Experts warn that the industry must address these growing concerns to maintain public trust.

previousArticles.title: Consequences: The Cost of Progress
previousArticles.summaryEn: This article examines how the massive energy demands of AI data centers affect the environment and household electricity costs. It discusses the conflict between technological growth and global net-zero carbon goals. Finally, it explores the socioeconomic impacts on local communities near large computing facilities.

previousArticles.title: The Growing Conflict Over AI Data Centers
previousArticles.summaryEn: New York state is moving toward a temporary ban on large data centers to protect its energy grid. This follows a national trend of local communities questioning the environmental and economic costs of AI infrastructure. Meanwhile, tensions are rising in other parts of the country as leaders and residents clash over these developments. The debate highlights the struggle between rapid technological growth and the needs of local citizens. Lawmakers are now seeking more transparency and regulation for the tech industry.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason for the rapid growth of Claude's user traffic between January and April?

options.A: It has surpassed ChatGPT in total monthly users.
options.B: It introduced features that allow the AI to operate without human intervention.
options.C: It is cheaper for businesses to implement than other AI tools.
options.D: It has received significant financial backing from the S&P 500 companies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Claude 的使用量成長迅速，是因為它推出了能讓 AI 在沒有人類協助下執行任務的工具。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到 ChatGPT 仍是擁有十億用戶的最受歡迎工具。選項 C 文中並未提及關於價格的比較。選項 D 雖然提到科技公司對 AI 的投資，但與 Claude 的成長原因無直接關聯。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between AI infrastructure and local communities?

options.A: Communities are generally supportive of data centers because they create new jobs.
options.B: The government has successfully mandated that all data centers be built in remote areas.
options.C: The physical needs of data centers often clash with the environmental and resource concerns of local residents.
options.D: Local opposition to data centers has successfully stopped all AI development in the United States.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段與第七段，數據中心需要消耗大量的電力與水資源，這與當地居民的利益產生衝突，導致地方組織試圖阻止其建設。選項 A 與文章描述的衝突情況相反。選項 B 文中並未提到政府有此類強制規定。選項 D 過於誇大，文章僅提到地方組織在對抗，並未表示 AI 發展已全面停止。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'this surge has turned the stock market into what some experts call a giant AI-focused structure', what is the best meaning of 'surge'?

options.A: A sudden and powerful upward movement.
options.B: A decline in quality over time.
options.C: A steady, slow decrease in volume.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，surge 指的是 AI 基礎設施投資金額從 7650 億美元快速增長至 1.6 兆美元的現象，意指「激增」或「猛漲」。選項 B 與 C 皆與「增加」的含義相反。選項 D 雖然在文中其他段落有提到關於 AI 的爭論，但 surge 本身是指數量上的快速變化，而非意見上的不合。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: AI is a perfect investment that will guarantee economic growth for all businesses.
options.B: The primary threat to the AI industry is the lack of competition among developers.
options.C: AI development faces significant economic, social, and safety challenges that could threaten its future success.
options.D: The history of technological resistance proves that AI will eventually be accepted by everyone.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章從經濟泡沫風險、競爭壓力、社會極端主義暴力，到資源消耗問題，全面探討了 AI 發展所面臨的嚴峻挑戰。選項 A 過度樂觀，忽略了文中提到的風險。選項 B 錯誤，因為文中提到競爭正在加劇。選項 D 雖然提到了歷史上的抗拒，但文章並未斷言 AI 最終會被所有人接受，而是強調若無法解決目前的負面問題，未來將充滿不確定性。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
